technical_terms_dropped: kṣudra (petty / small) — preserved by paraphrase as 'small weakness wearing the costume of a large thing'
verdict_reason: The engineering layer does not invoke a sectarian reading of klaibyam or kṣudra-hṛdaya-daurbalyam. It uses the consensus-of-traditions sense: this is faintheartedness wearing the costume of caution; the corrective is decisive movement. Both Shankara and Ramanuja agree on this functional reading; the verse is pre-doctrinal in the sectarian sense. The drop of klaibyam's gendered overtone is a defensible modernization, not a tradition collapse. No technical Sanskrit term that is doctrinally load-bearing has been silently dropped (uttiṣṭha is preserved, kṣudra is preserved by paraphrase). PASS.
